word: H6356 - פְּחֶתֶת - pᵉchetheth - pekh-eh'-theth - Fret inward

sections.paragraph:
Original: פּחתת

Transliteration: pechetheth

Phonetic: pekh-eh'-theth

BDB Definition:

  1. a boring or eating out, hole, hollow
    1. of leprous decay in garment

Origin: from the same as H6354

TWOT entry: 1761b

Part(s) of speech: Noun Feminine
